Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order Regarding Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFFS WILLIAM SORENSEN, AN INDIVIDUAL, and JEANNE SORENSEN's, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order Regarding Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Plaintiffs' motion terminating and monetary sanctions is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Within ten days of the date of this Order, Defendants shall serve verified amended responses and a privilege log in compliance with the Discovery Order of this Court dated July 5, 2023. Said amended responses shall be fully, completely and unequivocally compliant with the Discovery Order and the Discovery Act in all respects, including those identified in Plaintiffs' moving papers. The privilege log shall fully and completely identify all documents and other things withheld on grounds of privilege or shall state that there are none. Defendant Dolan shall verify said responses or shall forthwith submit an application for appointment of a guardian ad litem who shall verify the responses. Plaintiffs request an order for terminating sanctions. "[S]anctions are generally imposed in an incremental approach, with terminating sanctions being the last resort." Dept. of Forestry & Fire Protection v. Howell, 18 Cal. App. 5th 154, 191 (2017). There has not been a sufficient showing that less severe sanctions would not produce compliance with the discovery rules. August B. Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App. 4th 967, 992 (2009). The request for terminating sanctions is denied without prejudice to renewal in the event Defendants fail to comply with this order. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is denied in the interest of justice because sanctions were requested only against the individual Defendants, one of whom may be incompetent.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.
